title: EnsemblesGoogleDrive
rawIndexableTextContent: A Google Drive cloud backend for Ensembles. Overview EnsemblesGoogleDrive provides GoogleDriveCloudFileSystem, an implementation of the Ensembles CloudFileSystem protocol that stores sync data on Google Drive. It communicates directly with the Google Drive REST API v3 using URLSession — no third-party SDK required. Google Drive uses file IDs rather than paths internally. The backend transparently bridges between Ensembles’ path-based CloudFileSystem protocol and Google Drive’s ID-based system, caching folder and file IDs to minimize API calls. Authentication The included GoogleDriveAuthenticator handles the full OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow, stores tokens in the Keychain, and automatically refreshes expired tokens. Google Cloud Console setup: Go to Google Cloud Console and create a project (or select an existing one). Enable the Google Drive API under APIs & Services → Library. Go to APIs & Services → Credentials and create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID. Select iOS as the application type and enter your app’s bundle identifier. Note the Client ID (looks like 123456789-abc.apps.googleusercontent.com). Set the redirect URI to: com.googleusercontent.apps.YOUR-CLIENT-ID:/oauthredirect Code:  On subsequent launches, the authenticator restores tokens from the Keychain and refreshes them automatically. Call authenticator.isAuthorized to check if stored credentials exist. For apps that manage tokens externally, pass a static access token instead:  Setup  API Details The backend uses the Google Drive REST API v3 with the drive.file scope, which limits access to files the app creates. Ensembles paths are resolved to Google Drive file IDs via folder traversal, with results cached in memory. Operation Google Drive API Connect / Identity GET /about?fields=user File existence GET /files (query by name + parent) Create directory POST /files (folder MIME type) List directory GET /files (query by parent) Remove item DELETE /files/{id} Upload POST /upload/files (multipart) Download GET /files/{id}?alt=media  Error Handling HTTP errors are mapped to EnsembleError cases: 401 Unauthorized maps to EnsembleError.authenticationFailure. 404 Not Found maps to EnsembleError.fileAccessFailed. Other HTTP errors map to EnsembleError.serverError. Dependencies This module has no external dependencies beyond Foundation and the core Ensembles framework. All network communication uses URLSession.
summary: A Google Drive cloud backend for Ensembles.

title: authorize(presenting:)
rawIndexableTextContent: Performs interactive OAuth 2.0 authorization using ASWebAuthenticationSession. Opens the Google sign-in page, captures the authorization code, and exchanges it for access and refresh tokens.  The presentation anchor (UIWindow on iOS, NSWindow on macOS).

title: init(configuration:)

rawIndexableTextContent: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for Google Drive.  Overview Handles the full OAuth 2.0 authorization code flow: opening the browser, exchanging the authorization code for tokens, storing tokens in the Keychain, and automatically refreshing expired access tokens. Interactive authorization requires AuthenticationServices and is available on iOS 16+ and macOS 13+. Token refresh and Keychain storage work on all platforms including tvOS and watchOS.  Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. Mutable state (stored credentials) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by callers.
summary: Manages OAuth 2.0 authentication for Google Drive.
title: GoogleDriveAuthenticator

title: scopes
GoogleDriveCloudFileSystem
summary: A cloud file system backed by the Google Drive REST API v3 using URLSession.
rawIndexableTextContent: A cloud file system backed by the Google Drive REST API v3 using URLSession.  Overview This backend communicates directly with Google’s REST API — no third-party SDK is required. Authentication is handled via an OAuth 2.0 access token passed as a Bearer header on every request. Google Drive uses file IDs rather than paths to identify files and folders. This backend bridges the path-based CloudFileSystem protocol onto Google Drive’s ID-based API by walking the folder hierarchy to resolve Ensembles paths (e.g. ensembleId/events/file.json) to Google Drive folder and file IDs. Resolved IDs are cached in internal dictionaries to minimize API calls. The cache is invalidated when items are deleted or when not-found errors indicate stale entries. Two initialization paths are available:  Setup Go to the Google Cloud Console and create a project Enable the Google Drive API under APIs & Services → Library Create an OAuth 2.0 Client ID under APIs & Services → Credentials For iOS/macOS apps, choose “iOS” application type and enter your bundle ID Set the redirect URI (typically com.googleusercontent.apps.YOUR-CLIENT-ID:/oauthredirect) Configure the authenticator and file system:  The authenticator stores tokens in the Keychain and refreshes them automatically when they expire. The default scope is drive.file (per-file access), which limits the app to files it creates. Threading This class is @unchecked Sendable. All network calls go through a shared URLSession instance, which is thread-safe. Mutable state (caches, loggedIn flag) is accessed only from async methods that are serialized by the ensemble’s operation queue.
